title: Bridge Essay: Active Inference and Auditory Hallucinations.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.

**Active Inference and Auditory Hallucinations.**. Comput Psychiatr.. PMID: 30627670. DOI: 10.1162/cpsy_a_00022. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are often distressing symptoms of several neuropsychiatric conditions, including schizophrenia. Using a Markov decision process formulation of active inference, we develop anovel model of AVH as false (positive) inference. Active inference treats perception as aprocess of hypothesis testing, in which sensory data are used to disambiguate between alternative hypotheses about the world.
